mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher during FEDFUNDS-declining periods versus baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above recent lows signals labor market deterioration that erodes consumer confidence and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the return spread between triggered and non-triggered periods is statistically insignificant (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of declining retail sales and elevated CPI inflation signals stagflationary demand destruction with margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and CPIAUCSL shows no significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in predicting 5-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the combined signal underperforms its best single-proxy component.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, a rising rate combined with a falling unemployment rate signals a resilient economy absorbing restrictive policy, which reduces recession fears and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, the joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a hit rate greater than 55% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month acceleration in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and operating leverage, leading to positive earnings revisions and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO 3-month change does not positively predict 20-day forward returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent from the prior three-month level signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, foreshadowing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when HOUST declines exceed 10% over 3 months, or the signal's negative return spread is statistically insignificant (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of decelerating nonfarm payrolls and a federal funds rate above 4% signals restrictive monetary policy biting into labor markets, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, the interaction of slowing PAYEMS and high FEDFUNDS fails to produce negative 5-day returns with a win rate greater than 55% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth and retail sales growth together capture broad-based aggregate demand strength; higher concurrent readings in both series predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of GDPC1 and RSAFS does not produce positive 20-day returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
